title: Sintas Vel
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Vel}} {{Update|[[Star Wars: Blood Ties: Boba Fett is Dead]]}} {{Character |type=Bounty hunter |image=[[File:SintasVel-BFID2.png]] |name=Sintas Vel |homeworld=[[Kiffu/Legends|Kiffu]]''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'' |birth=[[34 BBY/Legends|34 BBY]], Kiffu''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]'' |death= |species=[[Kiffar/Legends|Kiffar]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]]''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=Black{{Tales|7|Outbid But Never Outgunned}} |feathers= |eyes=Dark blue |skin=Fair |cyber= |families=[[Clan Fett|Fett]] |parents= |partners=[[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] |siblings= |children=[[Ailyn Vel]] |affiliation=[[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Never miss me with the first shot. 'Cause you'll never get a second one.|Sintas Vel|}} '''Sintas Vel''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Kiffar/Legends|Kiffar]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kiffu/Legends|Kiffu]], who became a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] at a young age. It would be during her career as a bounty hunter that Sintas would meet fellow bounty hunter [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], whom she fell in love with and later [[Marriage/Legends|married]] when she was eighteen years old. The two lived together for three years on the world of [[Concord Dawn/Legends|Concord Dawn]], where Fett took a job as a [[Journeyman Protector/Legends|Journeyman Protector]] and Sintas gave birth to their daughter, [[Ailyn Vel|Ailyn]]. Their time together would come to an end when Fett's [[Lenovar|superior officer]] raped Sintas, leading Fett to kill the man and be subsequently [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] from Concord Dawn. Fett and Sintas divorced over the tumultuous incident, and Sintas was left to care for their daughter alone. Just prior to Ailyn's sixteenth [[Birthday/Legends|birthday]], Sintas accepted a job in order to buy her daughter a present, a job that would leave her trapped in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]] for close to forty years, until her granddaughter, [[Mirta Gev]], located her on [[Phaeda/Legends|Phaeda]]. Sintas was rescued and taken to [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], although she was initially blind and suffering from amnesia due to the years spent in carbon-freeze. With the help of her granddaughter, a regretful Boba Fett, and a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]-turned-[[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] by the name of [[Bardan Jusik|Gotab]], Sintas was able to regain both her sight and memory, allowing her to make peace with Fett and to get to know her granddaughter, Mirta. ==Biography== ===Youth=== {{Quote|I did love her. I just didn't know how to be part of a family.|Boba Fett, on his relationship with Sintas|}} [[File:BobaFamilyPortrait-BFID3.png|thumb|left|180px|A young Sintas with Boba Fett and their daughter, Ailyn]] Sintas Vel was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Kiffar/Legends|Kiffar]] born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kiffu/Legends|Kiffu]] in the year [[34 BBY/Legends|34 BBY]]. At some point in her youth, Sintas would take up the profession of [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunting. It was during her career as a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] that Sintas would meet fellow bounty hunter, [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]. The two fell in love and were [[Marriage/Legends|married]] in [[16 BBY/Legends|16 BBY]], when Sintas was eighteen and Fett sixteen years old. Though Sintas told Fett that he was too young to understand what he was doing, he replied that he only needed to know that she was beautiful, a terrific shot, and that he could trust her.''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'' They exchanged the simple, four-lined [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] marriage vow—though neither spoke [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']] at the time and didn't understand the words it entailed—and Fett presented Sintas with a [[Heart of Fire|heart-of-fire]] gemstone from Kiffu, which was believed to contain a part of both individuals' [[Soul/Legends|souls]] in the Kiffar culture, and that she had strung upon a necklace. Sintas and Fett settled on the agricultural world of [[Concord Dawn/Legends|Concord Dawn]], where Fett gave up his life as a bounty hunter to become a [[Journeyman Protector/Legends|Journeyman Protector]], one of the planet's [[Constable/Legends|constables]] charged with upholding the law. A year later, in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]], the young couple had a daughter they named [[Ailyn Vel|Ailyn]]. Around this time, however, Sintas was raped by [[Lenovar]], Fett's superior officer in the Journeyman Protectors whom Fett had considered a friend and mentor. Sintas never told anyone about being raped, not even Fett, though Fett would eventually find out the truth for himself. When he did, despite Sintas' pleas to the contrary, Fett killed Lenovar, a crime for which Fett was arrested, imprisoned, and tried. Because Fett didn't reveal the truth of Lenovar's own crimes to the [[Magistrate/Legends|magistrate]] in an attempt to protect Sintas, he was found unrepentantly guilty and [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] from Concord Dawn. Fett's exile and a series of bitter arguments and recriminations—including among them an instance where Fett demanded to know if Ailyn was really even ''his'' daughter—served to tear the couple apart and the two separated after three years of marriage when Ailyn was only two years old, leaving Sintas to care for her daughter alone. To this end, Sintas continued to work as a bounty hunter in order to support Ailyn. ===Assassination attempt=== {{Quote|I hear you're looking for me, cyborg.|Sintas meets Connor Freeman|[[Blood Ties: Boba Fett is Dead 2|''Blood Ties: Boba Fett is Dead'' 2]]}} [[File:Sintas_shoots.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Sintas shoots at the assassins.]] Years later, [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] sent [[Connor Freeman]], a clone relative of [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], to [[Concord Dawn/Legends|Concord Dawn]] to find Sintas and protect her from unknown assassins that were after Fett's family. Connor did manage to find Vel's home on Concord Dawn. While Connor was surprised to find Sintas' young daughter at the door, Sintas came behind him to confront him as to what he wanted. Before he got much of a chance to answer, a [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] strike team in a [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] began shooting at them, and Connor proved his good intentions by helping them dispatch them. Relocating to an armored safehouse in town, Sintas started telling him about how much he reminded her of Fett, and kissed him. As he was eating a late dinner afterwards, Connor discovered a family holo, discovering that he had just kissed his brother's wife.[[Blood Ties: Boba Fett is Dead 3|''Blood Ties: Boba Fett is Dead'' 3]] Confronting him about it, Sintas explained Fett's time as a [[Journeyman Protector/Legends|Journeyman Protector]] and how their marriage had played its course, with Ailyn getting up and making fun of them for kissing. Just then the safehouse door blew open, and Connor was able to deflect it from them which his cybernetic arm. As Governor [[Purton]] revealed himself as the man trying to kill Fett and his family, Connor and the Vels took cover from his grenades in the storage basement, with Sintas giving him a set of yellow [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]]. Purton then followed the grenades with a strike team, and they were able to force the governor into the basement with them. After killing his guards, Purton explained that he wanted revenge for Fett killing his son, [[Lenovar]], which prompted Sintas to tell him that Fett had killed Lenovar because he had viciously raped her, which Fett didn't reveal during the trial in order to protect her. Not believing her and calling her a liar, Purton attempted to strangle her, forcing Connor to shoot and kill him. Sintas then kissed Connor goodbye, explaining that Fett or anyone like him just brought her trouble. Unbeknown to her, Fett was standing by the trapdoor right then, watching his wife and daughter in silence. He then left with Connor, without revealing himself to Sintas.[[Blood Ties: Boba Fett is Dead 4|''Blood Ties: Boba Fett is Dead'' 4]] ===Later life=== {{Quote|Her worst days were when your daughter learned how to talk, and asked where ''Dada'' was.|[[Bardan Jusik|Gotab]] to Boba Fett, in 40 ABY|}} [[File:SintasVel1.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Sintas hunting for Pizztov]] At some point prior to [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], a canister containing a [[Hologram/Legends|holo-image]] of Sintas, Fett, and their daughter that belonged to Sintas was stolen by the criminal known as [[Pizztov]]. The only image of the three together as a family, Sintas tracked Pizztov to the city of [[Bidamount]] where Pizztov was attempting to use the canister to extort over six million [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] from Boba Fett with the threat of auctioning it off to the public in [[Eport|one of]] Bidamount's auction houses. Breaking through the floor of Pizztov's quarters, Sintas demanded the return of her property at [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] point, shooting one of Pizztov's bodyguards when he attempted to stop her. At the same time, Fett arrived, saving Sintas from a second guard's attempts to kill her, though he was looking to take the canister for himself. Pizztov fled and Sintas gave chase, eventually catching and shooting the man in the back; before she could claim her stolen property, Fett shot the canister from her hand. Pizztov attacked Fett with one of Sintas' [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]], though he missed and was killed by Fett. Wounded, Sintas pleaded with Fett not to take the canister, and Fett explained that the reason he'd fired was because the canister had been rigged to explode if not opened correctly. Upon opening the canister and seeing the holo of his family along with Sintas' tearful reaction, Fett decided to return the canister to Sintas instead of destroying it as he had originally planned. Though she was grateful, Sintas was also dismayed when Fett left her again, this time wounded. Despite this second abandonment, Fett's actions in recovering the hologram gave Sintas hope that he still in some way cared for her. [[File:Sintas_Armed.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Sintas and Boba Fett, both allied and opposed in their mission to re-acquire the stolen canister]] Just before Ailyn's sixteenth [[Birthday/Legends|birthday]] in the year 1 ABY, Sintas took a job pursuing a bounty for a large sum of credits. She planned to use the money to take her daughter to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in order to see [[Galactic City/Legends|the city]] and buy her some nice things for her birthday. Unfortunately, Sintas would never return home from her mission: she was captured and drugged with a sedative by a man looking to ransom her, possibly recognizing her as Boba Fett's ex-wife. When she didn't return, Ailyn believed Sintas to be dead and began to hate Fett, feeling as though it was Fett's fault for abandoning them. She became a bounty hunter, herself, and would devote her life to hating her father and make several attempts to find and kill him, even killing a [[Spar|clone]] she mistook for Fett{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}} and raising her own daughter, [[Mirta Gev|Mirta]], to hate Fett. Ailyn also managed to recover the heart-of-fire necklace that Fett had given to Sintas from the man believed to have killed her, eventually passing it on to Mirta as part of another attempt to kill Fett. When Ailyn was killed by [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], Mirta and Fett were able to establish an uneasy peace between them, and together attempted to find out what had happened to Sintas. During one evening at the ''[[Oyu'baat]]'' [[Tapcafe/Legends|tapcafe]] on [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], Fett and Mirta were approached by the elderly Mandalorian [[Bardan Jusik|Gotab]] who introduced himself as a Kiffar capable of [[Psychometry/Legends|reading]] the memories from the heart-of-fire gem Mirta had, in order to help shed light on Sintas' death. Gotab's insight and Mirta's further research led the pair to the planet [[Phaeda/Legends|Phaeda]] where they found Sintas not dead, but frozen in a slab of [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]] among the belongings of the dead [[Gangster/Legends|gangster]], [[Hidu Rezodar]]. Taking Sintas back to Mandalore, Fett and Mirta revived Sintas from carbon-freeze after nearly forty years with the help of Fett's [[Doctor/Legends|doctor]], [[Beluine]]. Initially, Sintas suffered from severe confusion, dizziness, sensitivity to sound, and blindness, and it soon came to light that she was suffering from amnesia as well, unable to recall large portions of her life or even who she was. [[File:Mirta_gev.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Sintas' granddaughter, Mirta Gev]] Though appearing to still be in her mid-thirties due to the carbon-freezing process, in her impaired state, Sintas was forced to rely on the kindness of strangers, living on Mandalore at the [[Beviin-Vasur farm|home]] of [[Goran Beviin]]—the closest person Fett had to a friend—and attended to by her granddaughter, Mirta, and Beviin's partner, [[Medrit Vasur|Medrit]]. Mirta returned her heart-of-fire necklace to her, and the others, including Fett, attempted to remind her of her past, but made little progress. It was only when [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]]—on Mandalore seeking training from Fett in order to deal with her [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] brother, Jacen—enlisted the help of Gotab, who was in fact not a Kiffar at all, but a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]-turned-Mandalorian well acquainted with [[Force healing/Legends|Force healing]], that any true progress was made. Gotab used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to restore Sintas' lost sight, and allow her to recover her lost memories. With her lost memory returned, Sintas swore to avenge the death of Ailyn if the opportunity arose, and was able to explain the circumstances of her and Fett's divorce to her granddaughter, revealing that she had never told Ailyn the truth or raised her to hate Fett, but that Ailyn had attempted to fill in the blanks for herself after Sintas disappeared and made Fett out to be more callous in her mind than in reality. When Mirta [[Wedding of Ghes Orade and Mirta Gev|married]] [[Ghes Orade]], Sintas attended the celebratory feast afterward at the home of Orade's father, [[Novoc Vevut]]. Sintas was also able to make peace with Fett, who was already remorseful for his role in leaving her and abandoning his daughter. He gave her another heart-of-fire gem, a rarer and more expensive [[Color/Legends|blue]]-colored one, along with a portfolio of [[Stock market|stock]] shares and properties he owned, explaining that she could either keep or sell them in order to allow Sintas to never have to hunt bounties in order to make a living again. Fett also informed her that no matter what her decision, she would be welcome to stay on Mandalore for the rest of her life. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|You can still say no. You know you have tragedy in your past.''"
"''Then I'll face it. Because it's part of who I am.|Gotab and Sintas, on getting her lost memory back|}} [[File:Sintas_the_Mandalorian.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Sintas in [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]].]] As a Kiffar, Sintas possessed the unique psychometric abilities of her people, along with a distinctive set of ''qukuuf'' facial [[Tattoo/Legends|markings]] in the form of three black stripes on each of her cheeks that had been presumably passed down from her mother in the Kiffar tradition.''[[Star Wars: Republic: Hidden Enemy]]'' Sintas was a proficient marksman, and proved herself capable of wielding two blaster pistols at once during her mission to recover the hologram that had been stolen from her by Pizztov. Dark-haired and blue-eyed, with high cheek bones and small pointed chin, Sintas was considered an attractive woman, though she wasn't one to rely on her looks in her career as a bounty hunter. Boba Fett once stated that "he could never get over how beautiful Sintas was." Though a hard and ruthless bounty hunter—willing to risk the life of an innocent bystander in order to reclaim the hologram of her family that had been stolen—Sintas showed a softer side around those she cared for, notably her daughter Ailyn, granddaughter Mirta, and lover Boba Fett, whom she called by the petname "Bo" even after they had divorced, and whom he referred to as "Sin" in return. ==Behind the scenes== The character of "Sintas" first appeared in the short [[Star Wars comic books|comic]], "[[Outbid But Never Outgunned]]" from [[Dark Horse Comics]]' ''[[Star Wars]] [[Star Wars Tales 7|Tales 7]]'', a story which primarily featured Boba Fett and a brief glimpse into what was, at the time, a mysterious past. Though the first twenty issues of ''Tales'' series [[canon]]icity was dubious at the time, Sintas' character was later canonized with appearances in both the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' article, ''[[Unusual Suspects]]'', and the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' article, [[The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors|''The History of the Mandalorians'']] where she's first identified as "Sintas Vel". She later featured in the [[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]] novel series, receiving mentions in both ''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]'' and ''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'' by author [[Karen Traviss]], before making a full appearance in Traviss' third Legacy of the Force novel, ''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'', where her character and history were further fleshed out. ==Appearances== [[File:Sintas_Face.jpg|thumb|right|125px|Sintas Vel, addressing Boba Fett]] {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Sintas Vel}} *[[Blood Ties: Boba Fett is Dead 2|''Blood Ties: Boba Fett is Dead'' 2]] *[[Blood Ties: Boba Fett is Dead 3|''Blood Ties: Boba Fett is Dead'' 3]] *[[Blood Ties: Boba Fett is Dead 4|''Blood Ties: Boba Fett is Dead'' 4]] *{{Tales|7|Outbid But Never Outgunned}} {{1st}} *''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'' *''[[Legacy of the Force: Invincible]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *{{GamerCite|6|Unusual Suspects}} *{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}} {{1stID|as "Sintas Vel"}} *{{Blog|abelgpena|13|Ailyn Vel, Daughter of Boba Fett}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{InsiderCite|117|Boba Fett: A Bounty Hunter's Journey|reprint=1}} *{{SW|url=news/so-uncivilized-great-gunslingers-in-star-wars-part-1|series=So Uncivilized: Great Gunslingers in Star Wars|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Sintas Vel |es=Sintas Vel |pl=Sintas Vel |pt=Sintas Vel |fi=Sintas Vel }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Vel, Sintas}} [[Category:Bounty hunters]] [[Category:Clan Fett]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Kiffar]]
